page: Microsoft Azure D4a_v4 is a general-purpose virtual machine belonging to the DAv4 server family. It is powered by an AMD EPYC 7763 processor utilizing the x86_64 architecture. The hardware configuration features 4 vCPUs mapped to 2 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled, providing 2.0 threads per core. The CPU operates at a clock speed of 3.525 GHz and is supported by 16.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. This instance includes 102 GB of bundled local storage, while offering no complimentary public IPv4 addresses and no GPU acceleration.

Performance benchmarks reveal a contrast between single-core and multi-core capabilities. The single-core CPU performance is strong, placing in the top 25% of stress-ng benchmarks, and Geekbench single-core scores are average. However, multi-core performance is comparatively weak, falling into the bottom 25% for stress-ng multi-core, Geekbench multi-core, and Passmark CPU tests. Memory bandwidth tests show average performance for large-block read operations at 40.46 GB/sec, but weak performance for small-block cached reads and large-block writes.

For specific workloads, the server achieves average results in single-threaded gzip compression, bzip3 decompression, and text processing. Conversely, database operations, Redis SET performance, and static web serving throughput are weak, placing in the bottom 25%. Large-scale parallel tasks, such as software compilation and ray tracing, are limited by the multi-core constraints of the 2 physical cores.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the D4a_v4 offers a balanced resource profile suitable for entry-level workloads that require dedicated CPU allocation without demanding sustained multi-core execution. It is best suited for general-purpose applications, light web serving, basic text processing, and small-scale development environments where single-threaded performance is prioritized over high-throughput database or parallel processing tasks.
description: Microsoft Azure D4a_v4 is a general-purpose virtual machine from the DAv4 family, powered by an AMD EPYC 7763 processor on the x86_64 architecture. It features 4 dedicated vCPUs across 2 physical cores, 16.0 GB of RAM, and 102 GB of bundled local storage. While single-core CPU performance is strong, placing in the top 25% in stress-ng benchmarks, multi-core performance and memory write bandwidth are relatively weak. The server delivers average performance for single-threaded compression and text processing, but exhibits lower throughput in database operations and static web serving. Qualitatively, this instance provides a balanced, budget-friendly resource profile for workloads that do not require intensive parallel processing. It is best suited for general-purpose tasks, light web serving, and development environments.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D4a_v4 is a general-purpose VM featuring 4 vCPUs, 16 GB RAM, and 102 GB local storage, powered by an AMD EPYC 7763 CPU. It offers strong single-core but weaker multi-core performance.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D4a_v4 is a general-purpose VM with 4 vCPUs, 16 GB RAM, and 102 GB local storage, powered by an AMD EPYC 7763 processor.
tagline: A balanced general-purpose virtual machine featuring dedicated AMD EPYC processing, bundled local storage, and strong single-core performance.
bullet_points: Powered by an AMD EPYC 7763 processor with 4 vCPUs and 16.0 GB of RAM
Includes 102 GB of bundled local storage for application data
Delivers strong single-core CPU performance but limited multi-core scaling
Features average performance for single-threaded compression and text processing
Best suited for general-purpose workloads, development environments, and light web serving
